heading: Studies by Comptroller General
label: 1143a Studies by Comptroller General
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Single-Employer Pension Plan Amendments Act of 1986, and also as part of the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985, and not as part of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 which comprises this chapter.
amendments: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Single-Employer Pension Plan Amendments Act of 1986, and also as part of the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985, and not as part of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States may, pursuant to the request of any Member of Congress, study employee benefit plans, including the effects of such plans on employees, participants, and their beneficiaries.

children.label: A
children.text: within the possession or control of the administrator, sponsor, or employer of and persons providing services to any employee benefit plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: which the Comptroller General or his representative finds, in his own judgment, pertinent to such study.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 3 Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the terms “employee benefit plan”, “participant”, “administrator”, “beneficiary”, “plan sponsor”, “employee”, and “employer” are defined in .section 1002 of this title

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: The preceding provisions of this section shall be effective on .April 7, 1986
